nuggets.headline: The iconic name "Daft Punk" emerged from an insult, but one half of the duo had disco in his blood from birth.
nuggets.text: Before they were Daft Punk, Thomas Bangalter and Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo were in an indie rock band called Darlin'. A British music journalist famously panned their music as "daft punky trash," which the duo amusingly embraced as their new name. Adding to their rich musical heritage, Thomas's father, Daniel Vangarde, was a successful disco producer in the 1970s, crafting hits like Ottawan's "D.I.S.C.O." and guiding their early career.
nuggets.imageCaption: Thomas Bangalter and Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo before their robot personas.

nuggets.headline: The repetitive, hypnotic groove of "Around the World" caught the ear of a funk legend who recognized its deep disco DNA.
nuggets.text: The track's iconic, looping vocal phrase, "Around the World," is repeated 144 times, creating a hypnotic effect that defines the French house genre. Funk and disco legend Nile Rodgers of Chic fame (who later collaborated with Daft Punk) described it as "breakdown music" when he first heard it, recognizing the duo's shared musical DNA for crafting repetitive yet utterly compelling grooves that you want to keep going.
nuggets.imageCaption: Disco legend Nile Rodgers, who deeply admired Daft Punk's "Around the World."

nuggets.headline: If that irresistible groove and catchy vocal hook of "Around the World" made you want more, you'll instantly connect with Modjo.
nuggets.text: Modjo, a French house duo, excels at crafting the kind of smooth, vocal-driven, and utterly infectious grooves reminiscent of Daft Punk's "Homework" era. Their global hit "Lady (Hear Me Tonight)" shares a similar upbeat tempo, funky bassline, and instantly recognizable vocal loop, making it a perfect next listen if you enjoyed the vibe of "Around the World."

nuggets.headline: Daft Punk's iconic robot helmets were more than just a costume; they were a prescient statement on the blurring lines between reality and fiction.
nuggets.text: Thomas Bangalter and Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo deliberately decided to embrace their robot personas on January 1, 2000, for their second album, 'Discovery.' Bangalter explained in 2001 that this choice reflected their view that 'The difference between reality and fiction is gone,' highlighting a world where altered images and fake news were becoming prevalent, making it hard to 'believe what you see on television any more.'

nuggets.headline: The mesmerizing, visually repetitive music video for 'Around the World' was brought to life by a director famous for his fantastical, mind-bending cinematic style.
nuggets.text: The highly acclaimed music video for 'Around the World,' featuring its unforgettable choreographed sequence of distinct groups representing the song's instruments, was directed by French filmmaker Michel Gondry. Known for his innovative and often surreal visual narratives, Gondry’s unique artistic vision made the video a standout, years before he would win an Academy Award for films like *Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind*.
nuggets.imageCaption: A still from the iconic 'Around the World' music video.

nuggets.headline: If the infectious, repetitive vocal hook and grooving bassline of 'Around the World' had you hooked, then you'll absolutely vibe with another French house duo who mastered the art of the perfect loop.
nuggets.text: Dive into Cassius, another seminal French house duo whose tracks like '1999' and 'Feeling For You' share that irresistible disco-infused energy and clever, driving rhythms that feel like a natural extension of Daft Punk's early sound. Their production style, full of funky samples and a consistent upbeat tempo, will keep your head bopping just like 'Around the World' does.

nuggets.headline: Believe it or not, one half of Daft Punk had serious disco credibility running in his family long before the robots took over.
nuggets.text: Thomas Bangalter's father, Daniel Vangarde, was a highly successful disco producer and songwriter in the 1970s, responsible for international hits like Ottawan's 'D.I.S.C.O.' and the Gibson Brothers' 'Cuba.' This deep musical lineage wasn't just influence; Vangarde actively helped the young duo navigate their early career, even assisting with their crucial deal with Virgin Records.

nuggets.headline: The hypnotic vocal loop of 'Around the World' isn't just catchy — it's a testament to extreme repetition, uttered a mind-boggling number of times.
nuggets.text: The song's iconic, robotic vocal hook, simply stating 'around the world,' is repeated a staggering 144 times in the album version, creating its mesmerizing auditory experience. Daft Punk themselves likened the creative process to 'making a Chic record with a talk box and just playing the bass on the synthesizer,' emphasizing the blend of disco, funk, and house with a minimalist, looped approach.

nuggets.headline: If the groovy, repetitive French house magic of 'Around the World' has you hooked, you absolutely need to dive into the iconic sounds of Cassius.
nuggets.text: This legendary French duo, featuring Philippe Zdar and Boom Bass, crafted their own brand of infectious, disco-infused house music that shares Daft Punk's knack for irresistible basslines and catchy, looped elements. Tracks like their classic '1999' capture a similar joyous, dancefloor-filling vibe, perfectly aligning with the spirit of *Homework*. [LAST.FM SIMILAR ARTISTS]
nuggets.imageCaption: The French house duo Cassius, known for their infectious grooves.
